2603.20656.title: Sinkhorn Based Associative Memory Retrieval Using Spherical Hellinger Kantorovich Dynamics
2603.20656.paper_summary: We propose a dense associative memory for empirical measures (weighted point clouds). Stored patterns and queries are finitely supported probability measures, and retrieval is defined by minimizing a Hopfield-style log-sum-exp energy built from the debiased Sinkhorn divergence. We derive retrieval dynamics as a spherical Hellinger Kantorovich (SHK) gradient flow, which updates both support locations and weights. Discretizing the flow yields a deterministic algorithm that uses Sinkhorn potentials to compute barycentric transport steps and a multiplicative simplex reweighting. Under local separation and PL-type conditions we prove basin invariance, geometric convergence to a local minimizer, and a bound showing the minimizer remains close to the corresponding stored pattern. Under a random pattern model, we further show that these Sinkhorn basins are disjoint with high probability, implying exponential capacity in the ambient dimension. Experiments on synthetic Gaussian point-cloud memories demonstrate robust recovery from perturbed queries versus a Euclidean Hopfield-type baseline.
2603.20656.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了一種用於經驗測量（加權點雲）的密集聯想記憶。存儲的模式和查詢是有限支持的概率測量，檢索是通過最小化基於去偏的Sinkhorn散度構建的Hopfield風格的對數和指數能量來定義的。我們推導出檢索動態作為球面Hellinger Kantorovich（SHK）梯度流，該流更新支持位置和權重。對流進行離散化產生了一個確定性算法，該算法使用Sinkhorn勢來計算重心傳輸步驟和乘法單純形重加權。在局部分離和PL類型條件下，我們證明了盆地不變性、幾何收斂到局部最小值，以及一個界限，顯示最小值保持接近相應的存儲模式。在隨機模式模型下，我們進一步顯示這些Sinkhorn盆地以高概率是不相交的，這意味著在環境維度中具有指數容量。在合成高斯點雲記憶上的實驗顯示，從擾動查詢中穩健恢復的能力優於歐幾里得Hopfield類型的基線。
2603.20656.author: Aratrika Mustafi et.al.
